mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals persistent labor demand and wage-supported consumption, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window, or the rank correlation between PAYEMS changes and 20-day returns is not positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% materially raises borrowing costs and discount rates, suppressing present values of future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, or the mean 60-day return during such periods is not statistically lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal coordinated strength across consumer spending and residential investment, generating a non-linear boost to aggregate demand forecasts and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of month-over-month RSAFS and HOUST increases fails to produce positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or a linear model without an interaction term produces equal or better directional accuracy than one including the RSAFS*HOUST interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production during periods of elevated consumer price inflation signal negative real supply shocks that squeeze corporate margins and trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of months when INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 3% annualized, or the rank correlation between INDPRO changes and 5-day returns in that regime is not negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects broad-based expansion in aggregate output and income, leading to upward revisions in earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth is followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is not positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals a deteriorating labor market that precedes weaker consumption and corporate pricing power, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Three-month increases in UNRATE greater than 0.3 percentage points are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the triggered subsample mean return is not below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a rising federal funds rate and elevated consumer price inflation accelerates the pace of financial conditions tightening, compounding discount-rate and margin pressures to produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: A model including the FEDFUNDS*CPIAUCSL interaction term does not significantly outperform an additive model in predicting negative 5-day S&P 500 returns (F-test p > 0.05), or the joint signal fails to produce negative average 5-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, supporting higher corporate operating leverage and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window, or the rank information coefficient between INDPRO changes and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03.
